title: Selysia prunifera
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Selysia prunifera. However, I can suggest a similar plant, Silybum marianum, also known as Milk Thistle, which is a plant with similar characteristics. 

Silybum marianum, also known as Milk Thistle, is a flowering plant in the thistle family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including liver protection and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of liver diseases and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Milk Thistle include silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids such as silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Milk Thistle include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support liver health and reduce inflammation.
